concerns.text: Tasks T014a, T020a, T021, and T022 explicitly include 'physical descriptors' (volume, atom count, mass) as features for the regression model. This violates FR-006 and FR-009 which strictly mandate using 'network metrics' as features. The spec does not authorize a model trained on physical descriptors, constituting a semantic weakening of the functional requirement.

Task T013a introduces a conditional log-transformation based on Shapiro-Wilk results, and T018b introduces a dynamic Bonferroni adjustment for n < 50. FR-004 mandates Pearson/Spearman analysis (implying standard application) and FR-005 mandates a fixed α ≤ 0.05. These tasks alter the defined scientific method without explicit spec authorization, violating the constraint preservation principle.

Task T023 specifies 'standard k-fold cross-validation (random splitting)' while SC-002 explicitly requires 'stratified cross-validation'. This is a direct contradiction in the validation methodology, weakening the success criterion defined in the spec.

Tasks T009a and T009b create a separate 'validate_dataset.py' script and dependency chain for dataset-variable fit (SC-003). The spec defines this validation as an integral part of the US-1 download/construction flow. Fragmenting this into a separate pre-check task with new dependencies alters the structural definition of the MVP pipeline without spec authorization.
